—This history we read in [Book]. Abundant riches and honor give large opportunities of doing good, but they are attended with many snares and temptations. People do not know much of Satan’s schemes and the deceitfulness of their own hearts when they covet riches with the idea of being able to do good with them. What can hurt those whom God will protect? What can shelter those whom God will destroy? Jehoshaphat was safe in his robes, Ahab was killed in his armor; for the race is not to the swift, nor the battle to the strong. We should be cautious of entangling ourselves in the worldly undertakings of evil men; and we should avoid engaging in their sinful projects even more. But when they call upon him, God can and will bring his faithful people out of the difficulties and dangers into which they have run themselves through sin. He has all hearts in his hand, so he easily rescues them. Blessed is the person that puts trust in the Lord.
